blocks.body: Present Perfect: Forms and Use — have / has + V3 for a result now, life experience, recent actions, and time up to the present moment.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In Present Perfect, we use have / has + V3 when we want to show a past action connected with the present moment.","rules":[{"label":"have / has + V3","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic affirmative model of the tense.","example":"I have finished. / She has cleaned the kitchen."},{"label":"No exact past time","color":"blue","text":"The tense works well when the exact past time is not important or not mentioned.","example":"We have visited Rome. / He has broken his phone."},{"label":"Connection with now","color":"amber","text":"The important point is not the past fact itself, but the result, experience, or period up to now.","example":"I have lost my keys. / They have lived here since 2021."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember the form for each group of subjects.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"have + V3","subtitle":"I have finished. / They have arrived."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"has + V3","subtitle":"She has started. / It has stopped."},{"label":"Regular and irregular","title":"worked / finished / gone / seen","subtitle":"After have / has, you need the V3 form, not V1 or V2."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"past action + link to now","subtitle":"A result, experience, recent action, or unfinished time up to now."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Present Perfect","sections":[{"label":"Result now","color":"emerald","description":"Something happened before, and we can see the result now.","examples":[{"en":"I have lost my wallet.","ua":"The result matters now."},{"en":"She has broken her glasses.","ua":"The situation is important now."}]},{"label":"Life experience","color":"blue","description":"Talk about experience in life without an exact time.","examples":[{"en":"We have been to Prague twice.","ua":"Life experience up to now."},{"en":"He has never ridden a horse.","ua":"No experience before now."}]},{"label":"Recent actions","color":"amber","description":"The action happened recently and still feels current.","examples":[{"en":"They have just arrived.","ua":"A very recent action."},{"en":"I have already sent the message.","ua":"The action is complete before now."}]},{"label":"Unfinished time up to now","color":"violet","description":"The time or situation started in the past and has not finished yet.","examples":[{"en":"I have lived here for three years.","ua":"The period continues now."},{"en":"She has had two meetings today.","ua":"Today is not finished yet."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form check","intro":"See how have / has works with V3.","rows":[{"en":"I have finished my work.","ua":"have + finished","note":"I + have + V3"},{"en":"She has lost her keys.","ua":"has + lost","note":"she + has + V3"},{"en":"We have visited London.","ua":"have + visited","note":"we + have + V3"},{"en":"He has just arrived.","ua":"has + arrived","note":"he + has + V3"}],"warning":"Do not say She have finished or I have went."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using V2 or V1 instead of V3.","wrong":"❌ I have went home.","right":"✅ I have gone home."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Mixing up have and has.","wrong":"❌ She have finished the task.","right":"✅ She has finished the task."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using this tense with a finished past time.","wrong":"❌ I have seen him yesterday.","right":"✅ I saw him yesterday."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Present Perfect = have / has + V3.","Use have with I / you / we / they.","Use has with he / she / it.","This tense is used for a result now, life experience, recent actions, and time up to the present moment.","After have / has, use V3."]}
